title: Austria-Hungary

infobox.value: **Court language:**  * [German](/wiki/Austrian_German "Austrian German")[[1]](#cite_note-i635-3)   **Cisleithania:**   * none (mostly *language of the land*: German)[[2]](#cite_note-y791-4)   **Transleithania:**   * [Hungarian](/wiki/Hungarian_language "Hungarian language")[[3]](#cite_note-5) * [Croatian](/wiki/Croatian_language "Croatian language") ([Croatia-Slavonia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Croatia-Slavonia "Kingdom of Croatia-Slavonia")) * [Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language") ([Fiume](/wiki/Fiume "Fiume"))

German, Hungarian, Croatian, [Czech](/wiki/Czech_language "Czech language"), [Polish](/wiki/Polish_language "Polish language"), [Ruthenian](/wiki/Ruthenian_language "Ruthenian language"), [Romanian](/wiki/Romanian_language "Romanian language"), [Bosnian](/wiki/Bosnian_language "Bosnian language"), [Serbian](/wiki/Serbian_language "Serbian language"), [Slovak](/wiki/Slovak_language "Slovak language"), [Slovene](/wiki/Slovene_language "Slovene language"), Italian, [Romani (Carpathian)](/wiki/Carpathian_Romani "Carpathian Romani"), [Yiddish](/wiki/Yiddish_language "Yiddish language"),[[5]](#cite_note-7) and others ([Friulian](/wiki/Friulian_language "Friulian language"), [Istro-Romanian](/wiki/Istro-Romanian_language "Istro-Romanian language"), [Istriot](/wiki/Istriot_language "Istriot language"), [Ladin](/wiki/Ladin_language "Ladin language"))

Two [constitutional monarchies](/wiki/Constitutional_monarchy "Constitutional monarchy") ([Austria](/wiki/The_Kingdoms_and_Lands_Represented_in_the_Imperial_Council "The Kingdoms and Lands Represented in the Imperial Council") and [Hungary](/wiki/Lands_of_the_Crown_of_Saint_Stephen "Lands of the Crown of Saint Stephen")) in [real union](/wiki/Real_union "Real union") ([dual monarchy](/wiki/Dual_monarchy "Dual monarchy"))[[e]](#cite_note-11)
[Charles I and IV](/wiki/Charles_I_of_Austria "Charles I of Austria")
* [House of Magnates](/wiki/House_of_Magnates_of_Hungary "House of Magnates of Hungary") * [House of Representatives](/wiki/House_of_Representatives_of_Hungary "House of Representatives of Hungary")
* [New Imperialism](/wiki/New_Imperialism "New Imperialism") * [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I")

infobox.key: • [Invasion of Serbia](/wiki/Serbian_Campaign_of_World_War_I "Serbian Campaign of World War I")

infobox.value: *[Gott erhalte, Gott beschütze](/wiki/Gott_erhalte_Franz_den_Kaiser#Use_in_national_anthems,_alma_maters,_and_hymns "Gott erhalte Franz den Kaiser")* ([English](/wiki/English_language "English language"): God preserve, God protect) | |

* [German](/wiki/Austrian_German "Austrian German")[[1]](#cite_note-i635-3)   **Cisleithania:**   * none (mostly *language of the land*: German)[[2]](#cite_note-y791-4)   **Transleithania:**   * [Hungarian](/wiki/Hungarian_language "Hungarian language")[[3]](#cite_note-5) * [Croatian](/wiki/Croatian_language "Croatian language") ([Croatia-Slavonia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Croatia-Slavonia "Kingdom of Croatia-Slavonia")) * [Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language") ([Fiume](/wiki/Fiume "Fiume")) |

| **Succeeded by** | | |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | [Austrian Empire](/wiki/Austrian_Empire "Austrian Empire") | | |  |  | | --- | --- | | [German-Austria](/wiki/Republic_of_German-Austria "Republic of German-Austria") |  | | [Hungary](/wiki/First_Hungarian_Republic "First Hungarian Republic") |  | | [Czechoslovakia](/wiki/Czechoslovakia "Czechoslovakia") |  | | [Slovenes, Croats and Serbs](/wiki/State_of_Slovenes,_Croats_and_Serbs "State of Slovenes, Croats and Serbs") |  | | [Kingdom of Yugoslavia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Yugoslavia "Kingdom of Yugoslavia") |  | | [Kingdom of Italy](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Italy "Kingdom of Italy") |  | | [Polish Liquidation Committee](/wiki/Polish_Liquidation_Committee "Polish Liquidation Committee") |  | | [Kingdom of Poland](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Poland_(1917%E2%80%931918) "Kingdom of Poland (1917–1918)") |  | | | |

and officially as the **Austro-Hungarian Monarchy**, was a multi-national [constitutional](/wiki/Constitutional_monarchy "Constitutional monarchy") [dual](/wiki/Dual_monarchy "Dual monarchy") [empire](/wiki/Empire "Empire") in [Central Europe](/wiki/Central_Europe#Before_World_War_I "Central Europe")[[h]](#cite_note-17) between 1867 and 1918. A military and diplomatic alliance, it consisted of two sovereign states with a single monarch who was titled both the [Emperor of Austria](/wiki/Emperor_of_Austria "Emperor of Austria") and the [Apostolic King of Hungary](/wiki/King_of_Hungary "King of Hungary").[[10]](#cite_note-18) Austria-Hungary constituted the last phase in the constitutional evolution of the [Habsburg monarchy](/wiki/Habsburg_monarchy "Habsburg monarchy"): it was formed with the [Austro-Hungarian Compromise of 1867](/wiki/Austro-Hungarian_Compromise_of_1867 "Austro-Hungarian Compromise of 1867") in the aftermath of the [Austro-Prussian War](/wiki/Austro-Prussian_War "Austro-Prussian War"), following wars of independence by Hungary (primarily [Rákóczi's War of Independence](/wiki/R%C3%A1k%C3%B3czi%27s_War_of_Independence "Rákóczi's War of Independence") of 1703–1711 and the [Hungarian Revolution of 1848–1849](/wiki/Hungarian_Revolution_of_1848 "Hungarian Revolution of 1848")) in opposition to Habsburg rule. It was dissolved shortly after [Hungary terminated the union with Austria in 1918](/wiki/Dissolution_of_Austria-Hungary#Dissolution "Dissolution of Austria-Hungary") at the end of [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I").

**Austria-Hungary**,[[g]](#cite_note-16) also referred to as the **Austro-Hungarian Empire** and officially as the **Austro-Hungarian Monarchy**, was a multi-national [constitutional](/wiki/Constitutional_monarchy "Constitutional monarchy") [dual](/wiki/Dual_monarchy "Dual monarchy") [empire](/wiki/Empire "Empire") in [Central Europe](/wiki/Central_Europe#Before_World_War_I "Central Europe")[[h]](#cite_note-17) between 1867 and 1918. A military and diplomatic alliance, it consisted of two sovereign states with a single monarch who was titled both the [Emperor of Austria](/wiki/Emperor_of_Austria "Emperor of Austria") and the [Apostolic King of Hungary](/wiki/King_of_Hungary "King of Hungary").[[10]](#cite_note-18) Austria-Hungary constituted the last phase in the constitutional evolution of the [Habsburg monarchy](/wiki/Habsburg_monarchy "Habsburg monarchy"): it was formed with the [Austro-Hungarian Compromise of 1867](/wiki/Austro-Hungarian_Compromise_of_1867 "Austro-Hungarian Compromise of 1867") in the aftermath of the [Austro-Prussian War](/wiki/Austro-Prussian_War "Austro-Prussian War"), following wars of independence by Hungary (primarily [Rákóczi's War of Independence](/wiki/R%C3%A1k%C3%B3czi%27s_War_of_Independence "Rákóczi's War of Independence") of 1703–1711 and the [Hungarian Revolution of 1848–1849](/wiki/Hungarian_Revolution_of_1848 "Hungarian Revolution of 1848")) in opposition to Habsburg rule. It was dissolved shortly after [Hungary terminated the union with Austria in 1918](/wiki/Dissolution_of_Austria-Hungary#Dissolution "Dissolution of Austria-Hungary") at the end of [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I").

Austria-Hungary was one of Europe's major powers, and was the second-largest country in Europe in area (after [Russia](/wiki/Russian_Empire "Russian Empire")) and the third-most populous (after Russia and the [German Empire](/wiki/German_Empire "German Empire")), while being among the 10 most populous countries worldwide. The Empire built up the fourth-largest machine-building industry in the world.[[11]](#cite_note-19) With the exception of the territory of the [Bosnian Condominium](/wiki/Austro-Hungarian_rule_in_Bosnia_and_Herzegovina "Austro-Hungarian rule in Bosnia and Herzegovina"), the Empire of Austria and the Kingdom of Hungary were separate sovereign countries in international law.[[12]](#cite_note-20)[[13]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTERoman2003[httpsbooksgooglecombooksidEvCfTIsTOskCpgPA401_401]-21)[[14]](#cite_note-22)

At its core was the [dual monarchy](/wiki/Dual_monarchy "Dual monarchy"), which was a [real union](/wiki/Real_union "Real union") between [Cisleithania](/wiki/Cisleithania "Cisleithania"), the northern and western parts of the former [Austrian Empire](/wiki/Austrian_Empire "Austrian Empire"), and [Transleithania](/wiki/Lands_of_the_Crown_of_Saint_Stephen "Lands of the Crown of Saint Stephen"), which corresponded to the medieval territories of the [Kingdom of Hungary](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Hungary "Kingdom of Hungary"). Following the 1867 reforms, the Austrian and Hungarian states were co-equal in power.[[15]](#cite_note-23) The two countries conducted unified diplomatic and defence policies. For these purposes, "common" ministries of foreign affairs and defence were maintained under the monarch's direct authority, as was a third finance ministry responsible only for financing the two "common" portfolios. A third component of the union was the [Kingdom of Croatia-Slavonia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Croatia-Slavonia "Kingdom of Croatia-Slavonia"), an autonomous region under the Hungarian crown, which negotiated the [Croatian–Hungarian Settlement](/wiki/Croatian%E2%80%93Hungarian_Settlement "Croatian–Hungarian Settlement") in 1868. After 1878, [Bosnia and Herzegovina came under Austro-Hungarian joint military and civilian rule](/wiki/Austro-Hungarian_rule_in_Bosnia_and_Herzegovina "Austro-Hungarian rule in Bosnia and Herzegovina")[[16]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEMinahan1998[httpsbooksgooglecombooksidRSxt-JB-PDkCpgPA48_48]-24) until it was fully annexed in 1908, provoking the [Bosnian crisis](/wiki/Bosnian_crisis "Bosnian crisis").[[17]](#cite_note-b1911-25)

Austria-Hungary was one of the [Central Powers](/wiki/Central_Powers "Central Powers") in World War I, which began with an Austro-Hungarian war declaration on the [Kingdom of Serbia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Serbia "Kingdom of Serbia") on 28 July 1914. It was already effectively [dissolved](/wiki/Dissolution_of_Austria-Hungary "Dissolution of Austria-Hungary") by the time the military authorities signed the [armistice of Villa Giusti](/wiki/Armistice_of_Villa_Giusti "Armistice of Villa Giusti") on 3 November 1918. The [Kingdom of Hungary](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Hungary_(1920%E2%80%931946) "Kingdom of Hungary (1920–1946)") and the [First Austrian Republic](/wiki/First_Austrian_Republic "First Austrian Republic") were treated as its [successors *de jure*](/wiki/Succession_of_states "Succession of states"), whereas the independence of the [First Czechoslovak Republic](/wiki/First_Czechoslovak_Republic "First Czechoslovak Republic"), the [Second Polish Republic](/wiki/Second_Polish_Republic "Second Polish Republic"), and the [Kingdom of Yugoslavia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Yugoslavia "Kingdom of Yugoslavia"), respectively, and most of the territorial demands of the [Kingdom of Romania](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Romania "Kingdom of Romania") and the [Kingdom of Italy](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Italy "Kingdom of Italy") were also recognized by the victorious powers in 1920.

## Name and terminology

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e9/5_corona_Franz_Joseph_1908.png/250px-5_corona_Franz_Joseph_1908.png)](/wiki/File:5_corona_Franz_Joseph_1908.png)

[Silver coin](/wiki/Silver_coin "Silver coin"): 5 corona, 1908 – the bust of Franz Joseph I facing right surrounded by the abbreviated legend "FRANC[iscus] IOS[ephus] I, D[ei] G[ratia], IMP[erator] AUSTR[iae], REX BOH[emiae], GAL[iciae], ILL[yriae], ETC, ET AP[ostolicus] REX HUNG[ariae]"

The [dual monarchy](/wiki/Dual_monarchy "Dual monarchy") has been anachronistically often called simply *Austria*,[[18]](#cite_note-eb9-26) referring to the main hereditary lands of the ruling [Austrian Habsburgs](/wiki/Austrian_Habsburg "Austrian Habsburg") and particularly to the [Austrian Empire](/wiki/Austrian_Empire "Austrian Empire"), which the dual monarchy emerged from.

The realm's full name used in internal administration was **[The Kingdoms and Lands Represented in the Imperial Council](/wiki/Cisleithania "Cisleithania") and the [Lands of the Holy Hungarian Crown of St. Stephen](/wiki/Lands_of_the_Crown_of_Saint_Stephen "Lands of the Crown of Saint Stephen")**

* [German](/wiki/German_language "German language"): *Die im Reichsrat vertretenen Königreiche und Länder und die Länder der Heiligen Ungarischen Stephanskrone*
* [Hungarian](/wiki/Hungarian_language "Hungarian language"): *A Birodalmi Tanácsban képviselt királyságok és országok és a Magyar Szent Korona országai*

Following a decision of [Franz Joseph I](/wiki/Franz_Joseph_I "Franz Joseph I") in 1868, the realm bore internationally the official name
**Austro-Hungarian Monarchy** ([German](/wiki/German_language "German language"): *Österreichisch-Ungarische Monarchie*, IPA: [[ˈøːstəʁaɪçɪʃ ˈʊŋɡaʁɪʃə monaʁˈçiː]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German"); [Hungarian](/wiki/Hungarian_language "Hungarian language"): *Osztrák–Magyar Monarchia*, IPA: [[ˈostraːk ˈmɒɟɒr ˈmonɒrɦijɒ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Hungarian "Help:IPA/Hungarian")),[[19]](#cite_note-27)

Other occasionally used names were the *Dual Monarchy* (German: *Doppel-Monarchie*; Hungarian: *Dual-Monarchia*), *The Double Eagle* (German: *Der Doppel-Adler*; Hungarian: *Kétsas*) or the *Danubian Monarchy* (German: *Donaumonarchie*; Hungarian: *Dunai Monarchia*), but none of these became widespread either in Hungary or elsewhere.

### k. u. k. monarchy

The state used internally the names *k. u. k. Monarchie* (English: k. u. k. monarchy)[[20]](#cite_note-28) (in detail German: *Kaiserliche und königliche Monarchie Österreich-Ungarn*; Hungarian: *Császári és Királyi Osztrák–Magyar Monarchia*).[[21]](#cite_note-29)

From 1867 onwards, the abbreviations heading the names of official institutions in Austria–Hungary reflected their responsibility:

* *k. u. k.* (**kaiserlich und königlich** or [Imperial and Royal](/wiki/Imperial_and_Royal "Imperial and Royal")) was the label for institutions common to both parts of the monarchy, e.g., the **k.u.k. Kriegsmarine** (Navy) and, during the war, the **k.u.k. Armee** (Army). The common army changed its label from **k.k.** to **k.u.k.** only in 1889 at the request of the Hungarian government.
* **K. k.** (**kaiserlich-königlich**) or [Imperial-Royal](/wiki/Imperial-Royal "Imperial-Royal") was the term for institutions of [Cisleithania](/wiki/Cisleithania "Cisleithania") (Austria); "royal" in this label referred to the [Crown of Bohemia](/wiki/Lands_of_the_Bohemian_Crown "Lands of the Bohemian Crown").
* **K. u.** (**königlich-ungarisch**) or **M. k.** (**Magyar királyi**) ("Royal Hungarian") referred to [Transleithania](/wiki/Transleithania "Transleithania"), the lands of the Hungarian crown. In the [Kingdom of Croatia and Slavonia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Croatia_and_Slavonia "Kingdom of Croatia and Slavonia"), the autonomous institutions used *k.* (**kraljevski**) ("Royal"), since according to the [Croatian–Hungarian Settlement](/wiki/Croatian%E2%80%93Hungarian_Settlement "Croatian–Hungarian Settlement"), the only official language in Croatia and Slavonia was [Croatian](/wiki/Croatian_language "Croatian language"), and the institutions were "only" Croatian.

## History

### Background and establishment
